_meta.name: test-bug
_meta.purpose: Claude classifies as test_bug — the test logic itself is wrong (wrong assertion value, off-by-one, incorrect expected text). App is fine.

output.human_explanation: The assertion expects 'Welcome, Admin' but the app correctly renders 'Welcome, admin' (lowercase). The application contract has always rendered the user role lowercased — the test was written with the wrong expected casing. Use @IgnoreCase or fix the expected value.
evidence: Application source (src/views/header.tsx) toLowerCases the user role before rendering — by design, present in the original commit
No other scenario in the suite asserts this string with mixed casing

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: The lowercased render is the original design; git blame shows it has rendered this way since the first commit
The selector targets the correct element — the text in it just doesn't match the asserted casing
